forbidden.title: This account does not have admin access.
forbidden.description: You are signed in through the SSO portal, but this account role is not allowed to open the admin frontend.

mfa_required.enrollment_title: Enroll multi-factor authentication before opening admin.
mfa_required.enrollment_description: Admin access is separate from the normal portal session. Admin accounts must have a verified second factor. Open the security portal, enroll MFA, then return to the admin frontend.
mfa_required.step_up_title: Your admin session needs to be refreshed.
mfa_required.step_up_description: The admin session is active, but admin access requires a fresh authentication window and MFA assurance. Sign in again through the SSO portal.

common.session_expired_desc: Login again from the portal to continue.
common.forbidden_desc: You do not have permission to view this page.
common.error_loading_desc: Try reloading or use the request ID for investigation.
common.error_generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.

dashboard.title: Admin Dashboard
dashboard.summary: Read-only summary for users, sessions, clients, audit, incidents, and DSR. The backend remains the security boundary.
dashboard.empty_description: No dashboard summary to display. Refresh data or check backend permissions if this condition is unexpected.
dashboard.error_title: Admin dashboard could not be loaded
dashboard.stale_banner: Latest refresh failed. Showing the last successful snapshot.
dashboard.degraded_banner: Some counters failed to load ({groups}). Showing available data.

policy.title: Security Policies
policy.summary: Security policy versions, activation evidence, and rollback history.
policy.error_title: Security policies could not be loaded
policy.empty_desc: No policy versions to display for this category.
policy.confirm_propose_desc: Create a new draft version of the {category} policy from this payload.
policy.payload_not_object: Payload must be a JSON object.
policy.propose_invalid: The payload was rejected. Review it and try again.
policy.transition_impact_replaces: Version {version} will replace the active version {active}.
policy.transition_impact_first: Version {version} will become the active configuration.
policy.error_invalid_transition: This version can no longer be activated.
policy.detail_desc: Policy version payload and activation actions
policy.active_none: No active policy for this category.

roles.title: Roles & Permissions
roles.summary: List of roles, permission matrix, and assignees for each admin role. Role changes require the admin.roles.write permission.
roles.matrix_desc: All permissions registered in the backend, grouped by domain.
roles.no_permissions: This role has no permissions yet.
roles.confirm_delete_desc: Are you sure you want to delete role {target}? This action is irreversible.
roles.confirm_sync_permissions_desc: Are you sure you want to update permissions for role {target}? This will instantly modify backend authorization claims.
roles.impact_users_affected: This changes the permission set for {count} users holding this role.
roles.self_affect_warn: You currently hold this role — saving this change can revoke your own console access.
roles.delete_failed_has_users: This role still has assigned users. Reassign them before deleting the role.
roles.delete_blast_radius: {count} users currently hold this role and will lose it.

users.title: Users
users.summary: Manage user status, MFA support, reset lifecycle, and session evidence.
users.error_title: Admin users could not be loaded
users.empty_desc: Create a new user or check filters and admin permissions.
users.create_user_dialog_description: Create a user identity and configure initial access.
users.create_user_partial_failure_title: User created, activation email could not be sent
users.create_user_partial_failure_desc: User was created but the activation email could not be dispatched. Use the Reset Password action in the Lifecycle tab to send a new activation link.
users.label_password_helper: Leave blank to send an activation/reset link to the user's email.
users.label_local_account_helper: Allow user login with email + password. If disabled, the user can only sign in via external SSO/federation and cannot reset their password.
users.validation_email_duplicate: Email is already used by another user.
users.validation_password: Password does not meet every security requirement.
users.confirm_lock_desc: This will lock {target} and may block sign-in.
users.confirm_reset_mfa_desc: This will remove MFA assurance for {target}.
users.confirm_password_reset_desc: This will issue a backend password reset workflow for {target}.
users.confirm_revoke_sessions_desc: This will terminate active session evidence item(s) for {target}.
users.password_reset_evidence: Password reset dikirim melalui channel aman backend. Gunakan audit evidence untuk pelacakan.
users.select_user_desc: Select a user from the sidebar list to inspect identity context, active sessions, and account lifecycle controls.
users.roles_self_warn: You updated your own roles. Please re-authenticate/login again to refresh your token claims.
users.roles_other_warn: Role changes will take effect after the user logs in again or the token is refreshed.
users.roles_min_required: Exactly one role must be selected.
users.role_assign_failed: Could not assign the role. Please try again.
users.role_step_up_desc: This change needs a fresh step-up (MFA assurance). Re-authenticate and try again.
users.assign_confirm_desc: This replaces the user's entire role and permission set. They keep only the role you select.
users.assign_confirm_self_desc: This changes your OWN role and permission set and can revoke your current admin access. You may need to re-authenticate afterwards.
users.roles_unavailable: The role list is unavailable right now.
users.create_user_success_desc: User created. Activation email queued for delivery to the user.
users.create_user_no_email_desc: User created. No email was sent.
users.reset_password_success_desc: Password reset link queued for delivery to the user's email.
users.reset_password_failed_desc: Password reset token was saved but delivery failed. Please try again.
users.not_found_desc: The account for this link does not exist or has been removed.
users.section_credentials_desc: Allow email + password sign-in. When off, the account only signs in via SSO/federation and cannot reset its password.
users.section_identifiers_desc: Optional. NIK/NIP/NISN and birth date are stored encrypted and only ever shown masked.
users.confirm_require_mfa_desc: The account will be forced to enrol multi-factor authentication before the next sign-in completes.
users.confirm_unrequire_mfa_desc: Multi-factor authentication will no longer be mandatory for this account.
users.update_profile_desc: Change identifiers and profile fields. Government identifiers are validated before submission.
users.actions_none: You do not have permission to manage this account.
users.create_failed_desc: The request could not be completed. Try again, and quote the support reference if you contact the team.
users.rate_limited_desc: Request limit reached. Wait a moment and try again.
users.step_up_required_desc: This action needs a fresh admin session. Re-authenticate and retry.

clients.title: OAuth Clients
clients.summary: Manage OIDC client metadata, redirect URI evidence, and secure secret rotation.
clients.error_title: OAuth clients could not be loaded
clients.no_client_selected_desc: Select or create an OAuth client to view governance evidence.
clients.empty_desc: Create a new client to start managing redirect URIs and scope evidence.
clients.create_dialog_description: Configure the OAuth client identity, type, redirect URI, and scopes.
clients.create_failed_desc: The request could not be completed. Try again, and quote the support reference if you contact the team.
clients.rate_limited_desc: Request limit reached. Wait a moment and try again.
clients.type_public_hint: Use for SPAs or mobile apps without a backend that can protect a secret.
clients.type_confidential_hint: Use for apps with a backend that stores secrets in a vault or server environment.
clients.client_type_backend_hint: This redirect URI looks like a server callback. Choose Confidential if the application has a backend and needs a secret.
clients.category_helper: Choose Staff for apps restricted to employees with an entitlement; Public apps are reachable by every signed-in user.
clients.validation_category: Choose an application category so a staff app is never published as public.
clients.scopes_hint: Enter one scope per line. The openid scope is required.
clients.validation_client_id: Client ID must be a 3-63 character slug using lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens.
clients.validation_redirect_uri: Redirect URI must be an exact HTTP/HTTPS URL without a wildcard or query.
clients.validation_logout_uri: Logout URL must be valid and use the same origin as the Redirect URI.
clients.validation_client_type: Choose a client type before creating the integration.
clients.validation_scopes: Select at least one scope and include openid.
clients.create_identity_section_desc: Define the display name and unique identifier for your OIDC client.
clients.create_config_section_desc: Configure application type, redirect URI integration, and data access scopes.
clients.client_id_helper: Only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens (3-63 characters).
clients.redirect_uri_helper: Exact HTTP/HTTPS callback address. Wildcards (*) or query parameters are not allowed.
clients.advanced_settings_desc: Optional configuration for advanced authentication lifecycle integration.
clients.logout_url_helper: Must be valid and use the same origin domain as the callback Redirect URI.
clients.label_delete_confirmation: Type client ID to delete permanently
clients.actions_none: You do not have permission to edit this client.
clients.rotate_secret_unavailable: You do not have permission to rotate this client's secret.
clients.confirm_rotate_secret_desc: The new secret takes effect immediately and the old secret stops working at once. Make sure the relying app is ready to receive the new value before you continue.
clients.secret_rotated_desc: Copy this secret now — it is shown only once and cannot be retrieved again.
clients.confirm_activate_desc: The staged registration becomes live and can begin issuing authorizations. No tokens are revoked.
clients.confirm_disable_desc: New authorization is blocked immediately and active tokens may be revoked. Existing sessions for this client can be terminated.
clients.confirm_decommission_desc: The client configuration is retired and redirect evidence is cleared. This cannot be undone from this console.
clients.confirm_delete_desc: The registration is deleted permanently and all of its tokens and sessions are revoked. Type the client ID to confirm.
clients.secret_hint: Rotating the secret reveals the plaintext only once. Copy it, then clear it from the screen.
clients.secret_reveal_warning: Store this secret securely in your key vault. It will not be shown again after you close the screen or press the button below.
clients.create_secret_warning: This secret is shown only once. Store it in a key vault or server environment and never commit it to Git.
clients.create_public_hint: Public clients do not have a client secret.
clients.create_public_recreate_hint: Need a secret for a backend app? Recreate this integration as a Confidential client.
clients.step_up_title: Re-authentication required to create this client
clients.step_up_description: Creating an OAuth client requires fresh authentication or stronger MFA assurance. Re-authenticate to continue.
clients.lifecycle_impact: Impact summary: disable blocks new authorization and may revoke active tokens. Decommission retires client configuration and clears redirect evidence.
clients.disable_hint: Disabling the client blocks new authorization requests and may revoke active tokens.
clients.disable_placeholder: Enter the reason for disabling this client...
clients.decommission_hint: Destructive action: permanently removes the client configuration. This cannot be undone.
clients.delete_hint: Highly destructive action: permanently deletes the client from the database. This cannot be undone.
clients.delete_confirmation_error: Type the client ID to confirm permanent deletion.
clients.not_found_desc: The client for this link does not exist or has been removed.

sessions.title: Sessions
sessions.summary: Inspect and terminate active session evidence across client contexts.
sessions.error_title: Admin sessions could not be loaded
sessions.empty_desc: No active sessions at this time.
sessions.terminate_hint: Terminating this session will immediately disconnect the user and force re-authentication.
sessions.self_affect_warn: This is one of your own sessions — terminating it will sign you out.

compliance.title: Audit Compliance
compliance.summary: Retention windows, audit/evidence exports, and the data-subject-request queue.
compliance.error_title: Compliance console could not be loaded
compliance.stale_banner: Showing the last successful snapshot — a background refresh failed.

retention.title: Retention status
retention.empty_desc: No retention windows have been recorded yet.

dsr.title: DSR queue
dsr.search_placeholder: Search by request or account code
dsr.empty_desc: No DSR has been submitted for the current filter.
dsr.review_desc: Approve, reject, or fulfill this data-subject request.

observability.title: Observability
observability.summary: Service health, queue and authentication metrics, recent events, and trace availability.
observability.empty_desc: No service health or events to display yet.
observability.dsr_confirm_approve_desc: Approving moves the request to the approved state so it can be fulfilled. Reviewer notes are recorded in the audit trail.
observability.dsr_confirm_reject_desc: Rejecting closes the request without fulfilment. A reason is required and recorded in the audit trail.
observability.dsr_confirm_fulfill_dry_desc: A dry run computes what would be exported, anonymized, or deleted without committing any change.
observability.dsr_confirm_fulfill_commit_desc: This commits the data-subject request and cannot be undone. Re-authentication may be required.
observability.dsr_legal_hold_notice: Blocked by an active legal hold — fulfilment cannot proceed.
observability.transport_error_no_ref: Backend service temporarily unreachable. Try again later or contact technical support.
observability.export_desc: Export filtered audit events to CSV or JSONL. Privileged action: backend requires step-up reauthentication and AUDIT_EXPORT permission.
observability.evidence_pack_desc: Compile a curated evidence pack (audit subset + integrity hash-chain + related DSR + retention) for a date range or correlation ID. Privileged action: requires step-up reauthentication and AUDIT_EXPORT, logging 'evidence_pack_generated'.
observability.evidence_pack_hint: Fill date range (From + To) or correlation ID to enable generation.
observability.retention_desc: Summary of retention windows, prune schedules, prune candidates, and last run for compliance evidence pack.
observability.search_desc: Search evidence by correlation/request code, session code, action, outcome, taxonomy, account, or date range.
observability.consent_desc: Quickly filter consent allow, deny, and revoke by subject or client to investigate consent trail.
observability.risk_challenge_desc: Backend auth audit events show login challenge outcomes, MFA-required states, and notification dispatch evidence when recorded.
observability.acr_policy_desc: Unknown ACR values are treated as no requirement (permissive) per accepted policy NG-03/FR-021. RPs requesting unrecognised assurance levels receive password-level flow rather than an error.
observability.consent_revocation_desc: Consent allow, deny, and revoke events are reviewed through the audit event feed.
observability.legacy_fallback_desc: Fallback usage stays an ops-tracked evidence item until backend emits a dedicated signal.
observability.token_lifetime_desc: Production token/session lifetime guard evidence remains backend-owned and deploy-gated.
observability.session_logout_desc: SID propagation, RP sessions, and front/back-channel logout outcomes use audit evidence.
observability.safe_error_desc: Admin evidence pages show reference codes and safe copy instead of raw backend/OIDC errors.
observability.transport_error: Backend service is currently unreachable. Submit incident code {ref} to the technical team for server log review.
observability.stale_banner: Showing the last known snapshot; the latest refresh failed.

auth_audit.title: Authentication Audit
auth_audit.summary: Authentication event history: login, logout, consent, MFA challenge, and authentication errors. Unlike the Audit Trail (admin actions), this records user authentication activity.
auth_audit.empty_description: Adjust the filters above or wait for authentication events to be recorded.
auth_audit.session_expired_desc: Sign in again from the portal to continue.
auth_audit.error_loading_title: Authentication audit could not be loaded
auth_audit.error_loading_desc: Reload, or use the request ID for investigation.

external_idps.title: External IdPs
external_idps.summary: Configure external identity providers, health, mapping, and failover controls.
external_idps.error_title: External IdP admin could not be loaded
external_idps.empty_desc: Add an external provider to manage health, mapping, and failover.
external_idps.form_client_secret_hint_edit: Leave blank to keep the current secret.
external_idps.field_pattern: Use only lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens, or underscores.
external_idps.create_invalid: A provider with that key, issuer, or metadata URL already exists, or the input was rejected.
external_idps.update_invalid: That provider could not be found or the change was rejected.
external_idps.preview_parse_error: Sample claims must be a valid JSON object.
external_idps.confirm_delete_desc: Permanently delete {name} and unlink every user federated through it. This cannot be undone.
external_idps.delete_invalid: That provider could not be found, or the change was rejected.

ip_access.title: IP Access Rules
ip_access.summary: Manage IP allow/blocklist rules for SSO admin access.
ip_access.error_title: IP access rules could not be loaded
ip_access.empty_title: IP access rules not yet available
ip_access.empty_desc: No IP access rules yet. Add a CIDR allow/block when permission is available.
ip_access.create_desc: Add an allow/block rule for a specific CIDR. Changes are audited.
ip_access.create_invalid: The rule could not be saved. Check the CIDR, mode, and reason.
ip_access.delete_invalid: The rule could not be deleted.
ip_access.confirm_delete_desc: Permanently remove the {mode} rule for {cidr}. This action is audited.

oidc.title: Protocol health and evidence
oidc.summary: Read-only view of Discovery, JWKS, availability, catalog, and issuer/endpoint consistency. The backend remains the source of truth.
oidc.error_title: OIDC Foundation could not be loaded

ops.title: Ops Evidence
ops.summary: Readiness, operational drill evidence, and compliance evidence references — without credential telemetry in the browser.
ops.error_title: Ops evidence could not be loaded
ops.drills_summary: Operational drills run through CI workflows and runbooks (not the admin API). Each card links the system of record and the official runbook to execute the drill and collect evidence.

profile.title: Admin Profile
profile.summary: The currently signed-in admin principal, read from the backend session. Read-only — no account changes are made here.

sso_templates.title: SSO Error Templates
sso_templates.summary: Customise the error screens users see when SSO sign-in fails. Changes are audited.
sso_templates.error_title: SSO error templates could not be loaded
sso_templates.empty_desc: The SSO error catalog has not been loaded.
sso_templates.edit_desc: Editing {code} ({locale}). Changes require re-verification and are audited.
sso_templates.action_url_hint: Optional. Must be an https URL.
sso_templates.edit_invalid: The template could not be saved. Check the title, message, and action fields.
sso_templates.confirm_reset_desc: Restore {code} ({locale}) to the system default. Your custom copy will be replaced. This action is audited.
sso_templates.reset_invalid: The template could not be reset.
